reasoning: The Lean signature asserts `∫ x in Icc 0 (π / 2), x * sin x = 1`, which is the Lebesgue integral of x·sin(x) over the closed interval [0, π/2] with respect to the volume measure. Since x·sin(x) is continuous on the compact interval [0, π/2], this equals the Riemann integral ∫₀^(π/2) x sin x dx. By integration by parts, this evaluates to 1, matching the informal claim exactly. The integrand, domain of integration, and claimed value all correspond directly to the informal statement. The unused `variable {f : ℝ → ℝ}` does not affect the theorem statement. There are no extra or missing hypotheses.
